page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz, utilizing 16 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provisioned via a bundled 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and memory subsystem performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier score of 3432.98, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also reaches the top 10% tier at 14.68 GB/sec. The PassMark memory score of 3027.88 is similarly positioned in the top 10%. Multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as the stress-ng multi-core CPU test (54911.92) and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression (116.52 GB/sec), perform within the average range. Large block memory bandwidth is strong, with read-only speeds of 286.73 GB/sec and write speeds of 159.68 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which can introduce resource contention compared to dedicated-core options. However, the inclusion of AMD EPYC Genoa architecture with AVX-512 and cryptography instruction sets (such as AES and SHA_NI) provides high instruction-level efficiency. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 500 GB local NVMe SSD deliver a balanced profile for compute-heavy tasks that require fast local storage but do not demand fully dedicated CPU cores. This server is suited for workloads such as web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and database operations that benefit from fast single-core execution and high memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of memory, a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and no GPU. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core metrics fall within the average range. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained high-utilization workloads, but the instance offers high instruction-level efficiency via AVX-512 and SHA_NI support. This server is suited for web serving, data compression, and database operations requiring fast single-core execution and local NVMe storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance.

tagline: An AMD Genoa server combining 16 shared vCPUs, fast local NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB local NVMe SSD
